title: What is In-Context Learning?
concept: In-context learning is the ability of an LLM to perform a task simply by reading examples in the prompt, without updating any weights.

exercises.prompt: What do we call it when an AI learns a task on the fly just by reading examples in-prompt fly just by looking at examples inside the chat?
exercises.explain: In-context learning is the amazing ability of modern AIs to learn how to do a task on the spot, just by reading examples you give them in the chat box.

cards.title: Learning Without Changing
cards.body: Imagine teaching a friend a brand new card game just by playing one practice round. They don't need brain surgery to learn the rules; they just look at your cards and get it. AI can do the exact same thing!

cards.title: What is In-Context Learning?
cards.body: In-Context Learning (ICL) is when an AI learns how to do a task simply by looking at the examples you type into the prompt. It figures out the pattern on the fly without changing its core programming or permanent memory.

cards.title: The Cheat Sheet Chef
cards.body: Think of a waiter who is handed a cheat sheet of today's specials right before they take your order. The waiter doesn't go back to school to learn the new menu; they just read it off the sheet, use the information instantly, and forget it once the table leaves.

cards.title: Seeing is Learning
cards.body:
If you ask an AI:
"Predict sentiment.
'I love sunny days' -> Positive
'I hate rain' -> Negative
'This pizza is okay' -> "

The AI will output "Neutral". It learned how to format its response just by looking at your quick examples!

cards.title: Temporary, Not Permanent!
cards.body: Some people think that when an AI learns from your prompt examples, it is updating its permanent brain (weights). That is a myth! Once you close the chat, the AI completely forgets those examples. It only uses them "in the moment."

cards.title: Two Ways to 'Learn'
cards.body: Let's compare standard training (updating the brain) with in-context learning (reading a temporary note).

left.text: Permanently rewires the AI's weights by training it on a huge dataset.

right.text: Temporarily guides the AI using examples typed directly inside the prompt.

exercises.prompt: How does a Large Language Model perform a task using in-context learning?

options.text: By downloading a new update from the app store.
By reading examples provided directly in the prompt.

exercises.explain: In-context learning allows the AI to pick up new tasks instantly from the prompt without needing any updates to its internal weights.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In-context learning changes the permanent weights of an AI model.
exercises.explain: In-context learning is temporary. The model's permanent weights remain exactly the same throughout the process.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the explanation of where in-context learning takes place.
exercises.sentence: In-context learning happens when the AI reads examples directly inside the current [blank].
exercises.explain: In-context learning happens entirely inside the text prompt you send to the AI. No weights or files are updated.

exercises.prompt: Which prompt relies on in-context learning to show the AI how to format its output?

options.text: Prompt B: What is a banana? Explain in detail.
Prompt C: Write a short story about a talking banana.

exercises.explain: Prompt A uses in-context learning by showing the model input-output examples ('apple' -> 'fruit', 'carrot' -> 'vegetable') to teach it the format before asking it to label 'banana'.

exercises.prompt: If you give an AI model three examples of how to translate French slang in a chat, will it permanently remember how to do this in a brand new chat session tomorrow?

options.text: No, because the model's weights were not updated; it only used the examples temporarily.
Yes, because the model permanently updates its weights after reading examples.
Yes, because the prompt is permanently saved into the model's brain.
No, because the model ignores all instructions inside the prompt.

exercises.explain: In-context learning is temporary and happens entirely within the prompt window. Once the conversation is closed, the model forgets the examples because its permanent weights were never modified.
